reasoning: The Treasury General Account (TGA) tends to fluctuate around the Treasury's target balance, typically set between $750B and $850B. The latest known balance of ~$905B in late October 2025 is quite high, suggesting a tendency for mean-reversion downward as the Treasury trims cash buffers or allows seasonal outflows to exceed inflows. The mid-November and early December periods (prior to the major mid-December corporate tax receipts) are usually characterized by tax-poor weeks where spending and mid-month debt coupon payments operate to drain the TGA. This strongly indicates a downward drift for the first two resolution dates in comparison to early November. Consequently, the probability of an increase is estimated to be below 50%. In line with the principle that uncertainty grows over longer horizons, predictions are anchored below the 50% mark but gradually revert toward 0.50 (maximum uncertainty) for the more distant February 2026 horizon, when multiple intermediate tax influx dates (Dec 15, Jan 15) and spending fluctuations make the exact path much harder to chart confidently.
